content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed `master...HEAD` excluding `.ralph-burning/` and ran targeted iterative-workflow tests; the branch is close, but I found three fix-now issues in the new `iterative_minimal` config/engine paths.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: `config set` / `config set-project` can persist an invalid `iterative_minimal` policy whenever `max_consecutive_implementer_rounds` and `stable_rounds_required` are split across workspace and project scopes. affected_files: `src/contexts/workspace_governance/config.rs:561-573`, `src/contexts/workspace_governance/config.rs:584-600`, `src/contexts/workspace_governance/config.rs:2208-2265`. The setters only validate each TOML file in isolation, so a workspace `max_consecutive_implementer_rounds = 5` plus a project `stable_rounds_required = 6` is accepted even though the next merged `EffectiveConfig::load_for_project()` will fail. The same bug exists in reverse for workspace edits against existing project overrides. remediation_hint: After applying an edit, validate the resolved effective policy for the affected scope(s) instead of only calling `validate_iterative_minimal_settings()` on the individual documents.
   Rationale: This creates self-inflicted broken configs that only surface on later loads, which is a real correctness and robustness issue for the new nested settings.
2. finding_summary: Iterative implementer invocations never receive the current loop iteration, so every stabilization pass is dispatched with the same agent-visible request context. affected_files: `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7026-7042`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7776-7808`. The new preset is supposed to call the implementer with `(prompt, amendments, iteration)`, but the request only carries `cycle`, `attempt`, `completion_round`, and pending amendments, while `attempt_number` also stays constant for the whole stage attempt. remediation_hint: Thread `iteration` (and ideally the loop limits) into the iterative `InvocationPayload.context` so the backend can distinguish first vs later stabilization passes.
   Rationale: Without round metadata, the backend cannot adapt behavior across iterations; in practice the loop degenerates into repeated identical calls, which undermines the core semantics of the new `iterative_minimal` flow.
3. finding_summary: A stale `ImplementerLoopExited` event suppresses the real exit record when resume rewinds and re-runs the terminal iteration. affected_files: `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6111-6127`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6437-6453`, `src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6982-6995`. If the terminal sidecar is missing, resume rewinds state and re-invokes the last iteration, but any previously written loop-exited event is left in place. Later exit recording only checks whether any matching exit event already exists for the run/cursor/attempt, so a stale `reason` / `total_iterations` from the abandoned terminal attempt prevents the correct exit event from ever being appended. remediation_hint: Clear or ignore prior loop-exited records when rewinding, or make `iterative_loop_exit_recorded()` verify that the existing event matches the current terminal iteration before suppressing a new append.
   Rationale: The durable journal becomes inaccurate after a rewind, which breaks the promised per-iteration audit trail and can mislead `run tail` and later resume diagnostics about why the loop actually terminated.
